title: CSE 160 — Computer Graphics (UCSC, Winter 2021)
description: WebGL graphics course at UCSC, Winter 2021. Five interactive demos: 2D drawing, hierarchical transforms, articulated 3D character, textures + animated video skybox, and a fully-lit final scene. All demos are vanilla JS + WebGL 1.0 with hand-rolled transformation matrices (no three.js). The 70MB galaxy video texture in asg3/asg4 is re-encoded to ~13MB at 720×720 by a build-script step in CI.

assets.title: Asg 0 — 2D Drawing
assets.description: Click-and-drag 2D primitives on a canvas with WebGL. Sets up the rendering pipeline, gl-matrix transforms, and basic event-driven drawing.

assets.title: Asg 1 — Shapes & Transformations
assets.description: Interactive shapes with transformation matrix demonstrations — translate, rotate, scale a hierarchy of triangles + squares using gl-matrix.

assets.title: Asg 2 — Derpy Charmeleon (3D Articulated Character)
assets.description: Fully-articulated 3D Charmeleon. Drag to rotate camera, scroll to zoom, slide controls to pose the limbs and waggle the tail. Hierarchy of joints with parent-relative transforms, animation toggle.

assets.title: Asg 3 — Textures, Skybox & Animated Galaxy
assets.description: 3D scene with applied textures (mushrooms, dirt, mycelium) and a skybox sphere with an animated galaxy video texture playing on it. Drag to look around, click textures cycle. Per-vertex normals, multi-texture sampling, video-as-texture.

assets.title: Asg 4 — Lighting, Shadows & Final Scene
assets.description: Final assignment: full Phong/Blinn-Phong lighting, point lights, ambient + diffuse + specular components, with the textured world and animated galaxy skybox from asg3. The most ambitious demo — touches every prior assignment's concepts.
